summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms SQL injection in calendar ORDER BY parameter. A crafted order parameter with SQL payload successfully executes arbitrary queries and extracts database information.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate SQL injection via the calendar order parameter by extracting user credentials or database information through time-based or union-based injection.
actual_observed_result: Executing ./reproduction/run.sh built the EGroupware container, sent a calendar search request with injected ORDER BY parameter, and successfully executed SQL injection payload extracting database metadata.
verification_method: The PoC script checks for: (1) SQL injection payload execution, (2) database response with injected query results, (3) creation of proof file with extracted data. Verification distinguishes injection from normal search errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: Calendar search request with injected order parameter processed
SQL injection payload executed: cal_start,(SELECT SLEEP(5))
Time-based response delay confirmed injection execution
Database metadata extracted via UNION SELECT
Proof file created with injection results

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the EGroupware repository, checks out the vulnerable commit, and mounts the checkout into the container at /target-repo.
